initial_request_score.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: AFM-RL: Large Protein Complex Docking Using AlphaFold-Multimer and Reinforcement Learning
AFM-RL was benchmarked on a dataset of 57 protein complexes with six to twenty (6-20) chains.

Here, we expand the ability of this method to predict the structures of large protein complexes with six to twenty chains. We use AlphaFold-Multimer (AFM) to predict pairwise models and then assemble them using our reinforcement learning framework.
The average root mean square deviation (RMSD) and TM-SCORE of the best-assembled model across all targets are 10.09 Å and 0.81, respectively.

issues.claim: Average RMSD of 10.09 Å and TM-SCORE of 0.81
issues.reason: These values are reported as experimental results in the Results section, not as pre-defined initial goals. Scoring rule D penalizes treating experimental results as initial goals.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Change quantifiable_goal to describe the aim for 'improved modeling performance' or 'lower RMSD compared to existing methods' without specifying the exact result values beforehand.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: We evaluated AFM-RL on the same dataset as MoLPC.
We selected complexes with 6 to 15 chains... We have 57 complexes in total.

issues.reason: The text states the dataset is the 'same dataset as MoLPC', which corresponds to Reference [23] (Bryant et al., MoLPC). The extraction missed this citation.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add Reference [23] to the references list for Step 1 to acknowledge the MoLPC dataset source.
